ops.title: Claude Code 2.1.132→2.1.210 orchestrator audit: no action needed; two overlap FYIs
ops.body:
Audited 2026-07-15 against installed Claude Code 2.1.210. Every flag the spawn path passes (`--session-id`, `--resume`, `--mcp-config`, `--strict-mcp-config`, `--settings`, `--append-system-prompt`; orchestrate/spawn.go) is present and stable, with no deprecations. The SessionStart hook we inject is already `type: "command"` (2.1.207 made non-command hooks a hard error there). The transcript tailer reads only `type`/`isSidechain`/`message`, untouched by 2.1.208's transcript slimming. `--session-id` still requires a UUID; `--fork-session` exists and our "never --fork-session" resume design remains correct.

Strategic FYIs to re-check periodically:
1. Native background-agent orchestration matured this window: `claude agents` dashboard, `--bg/--background`, JSON state output, `--cwd` scoping, auto commit+push+draft-PR (2.1.198). First-party overlap with cc-orchestrate's spawn+supervise+status loop — periodically re-evaluate riding it vs our backend layer.
2. Native `-w/--worktree` and `--tmux` flags: claude can now create its own worktree and tmux session — conceptual overlap with worktree/ and the tmux driver (only tmux, so no action while we support 5 backends).
